---
title: "support network map"
format: html
---

## Support Network Map

Recovery support works best when roles are clear. Different people can help with different needs: cravings, accountability, practical help, or emotional support.

Planning outreach ahead of time reduces hesitation during high-risk moments.

Support is most effective when it is specific, timely, and easy to access. In high-risk moments, vague plans can lead to delay, while clear roles and contact plans make help more available.

Strengthening support structure reduces isolation and improves accountability. It also helps you recover faster after setbacks by reconnecting you to people and routines that stabilize recovery.

::: callout
### Instructions
List at least one contact for each category and choose a specific time to reach out this week.
:::

| Support need | Person / group | How to contact | When I will reach out |
|---|---|---|---|
| Craving support | | | |
| Emotional support | | | |
| Practical support | | | |
| Recovery accountability | | | |
